title: Skillet Creamy Chicken and Parmesan Gnocchi
subtitle: Pan-seared chicken in a white wine cream sauce with kale and gnocchi, finished in the oven.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: One oven-safe skillet from stovetop to oven. Bacon goes on top at the end for crunch.
items: ½ cup grated parmesan, plus more for serving
ingredients.callout: Key tip: Gnocchi cooks raw right in the sauce—no boiling needed. Add it in, cook until the pieces float, then bake.

body.html: Dredged chicken seared in a bacon-fat skillet, then a white wine cream sauce built in the same pan—kale and raw gnocchi cook directly in the sauce. Everything goes into the oven together for a bubbling, parmesan-crusted finish.

items: Crisp bacon with rosemary in the skillet, set aside
Sear chicken in butter until golden, set aside
Add kale + raw gnocchi, cook until gnocchi float
Nestle chicken back in, top with parmesan, bake 10 min

body.html: Key move: Use an oven-safe skillet (cast iron or stainless). The gnocchi goes in raw—it cooks in the sauce and finishes in the oven.

slides.title: Season + Dredge Chicken
slides.checkboxLabel: Season chicken, dredge in flour mixture

items: Season chicken all over with salt and pepper.
In a shallow bowl, combine flour, paprika, cayenne, and 1 tbsp thyme.
Dredge each piece through the flour mixture and toss to coat.

body.html: Shake off excess: A thin, even coating is what you want—too much flour will clump in the sauce later.

slides.title: Crisp the Bacon
slides.checkboxLabel: Bacon crisp, rosemary bloomed, set aside

items: In an oven-safe skillet, cook bacon over medium heat until crisp, ~5 minutes.
During the last minute, add the rosemary—it will sizzle and crisp in the fat.
Remove bacon to a plate. Leave the fat in the pan.

body.html: Don't drain the fat: It carries the rosemary flavor and helps sear the chicken. The sauce absorbs it.

slides.title: Sear the Chicken
slides.checkboxLabel: Chicken golden on both sides, set aside

items: Add 1 tbsp butter to the skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the dredged chicken. Sear 3–5 minutes per side until golden.
Add another 1 tbsp butter and let it brown around the chicken, ~2 minutes.
Remove chicken to a plate. It won't be fully cooked yet—that's fine.

body.html: Don't cook through: Chicken finishes in the oven. You're just building the crust and flavor here.

slides.title: Build Sauce + Bake
slides.checkboxLabel: Sauce built, gnocchi floating, baked 10 min

items: Add 1 tbsp butter to the skillet. Add shallots, garlic, 1 tbsp thyme, and a pinch each of salt and pepper. Cook briefly.
Add the wine, then the broth. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add kale and raw gnocchi. Cook ~5 minutes, stirring gently, until gnocchi float.
Slide the chicken back into the skillet.
Transfer to the oven. Cook uncovered 10 minutes, until sauce is bubbling around the chicken.

body.html: Gnocchi cue: Floating = done. Don't let them sit in the sauce too long before the oven or they'll get mushy.

items: Spoon chicken, gnocchi, and sauce into bowls or onto plates.
Top with the crispy bacon and rosemary.

body.html: YUM. The sauce should be creamy and cling to the gnocchi—not watery. The oven time tightens it up perfectly.
